tool.name: Tiberius
tool.description: deep learning-based ab initio gene structure prediction
tool.command:
ln -s '$genome' ./genome.fa &&
TF_NUM_INTRAOP_THREADS="\${GALAXY_SLOTS}" tiberius.py --genome genome.fa --model_cfg '${model_cfg.fields.model_cfg}' --out output.gtf --batch_size '$batch_size'
#if $model_cfg.fields.softmasking == 'False': --no_softmasking #end if

param.name: softmasked
param.label: Has your genome been soft-masked?
param.help: The Tiberius developers recommend using unmasked models. Some models that were trained on soft-masked genomes are avaialable, but these are only appropriate for finding genes in genomes that were soft-masked in the same way as the training data.

tool.help:
`Tiberius <https://github.com/Gaius-Augustus/Tiberius>`__ is a deep learning-based ab initio gene structure prediction tool. It predicts genes from genomic sequence only.
To run Tiberius, you need to provide a FASTA file containing the genomic sequences and choose a pre-trained model.
More information about the models is available on the Tiberius GitHub `page <https://github.com/Gaius-Augustus/Tiberius/tree/main/model_cfg>`__
Tiberius end-to-end integrates convolutional and long short-term memory layers with a differentiable HMM layer. It can be used to predict gene structures from genomic sequences only (ab initio), while matching the accuracy of tools that use extrinsic evidence.
